2603.16751.title: Finding Common Ground in a Sea of Alternatives
2603.16751.paper_summary: We study the problem of selecting a statement that finds common ground across diverse population preferences. Generative AI is uniquely suited for this task because it can access a practically infinite set of statements, but AI systems like the Habermas machine leave the choice of generated statement to a voting rule. What it means for this rule to find common ground, however, is not well-defined. In this work, we propose a formal model for finding common ground in the infinite alternative setting based on the proportional veto core from social choice. To provide guarantees relative to these infinitely many alternatives and a large population, we wish to satisfy a notion of proportional veto core using only query access to the unknown distribution of alternatives and voters. We design an efficient sampling-based algorithm that returns an alternative in the (approximate) proportional veto core with high probability and prove matching lower bounds, which show that no algorithm can do the same using fewer queries. On a synthetic dataset of preferences over text, we confirm the effectiveness of our sampling-based algorithm and compare other social choice methods as well as LLM-based methods in terms of how reliably they produce statements in the proportional veto core.
2603.16751.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究選擇一個能在多樣化人口偏好中找到共同立場的聲明的問題。生成式AI特別適合這項任務，因為它可以訪問幾乎無限的聲明集，但像哈貝馬斯機器這樣的AI系統將生成聲明的選擇留給投票規則。然而，這個規則如何找到共同立場並不明確。在這項工作中，我們提出了一個基於社會選擇中比例否決核心的形式模型，用於在無限替代方案的情境中找到共同立場。為了在這些無限多的替代方案和龐大的人口中提供保證，我們希望僅使用對未知替代方案和選民分佈的查詢訪問來滿足比例否決核心的概念。我們設計了一個高效的基於抽樣的算法，該算法以高概率返回（近似）比例否決核心中的一個替代方案，並證明了匹配的下界，顯示沒有算法可以使用更少的查詢做到這一點。在一個關於文本的偏好的合成數據集中，我們確認了我們的基於抽樣的算法的有效性，並比較了其他社會選擇方法以及基於LLM的方法在多大程度上可靠地產生比例否決核心中的聲明。
2603.16751.author: Jay Chooi et.al.
2603.16751.authors: Jay Chooi, Paul Gölz, Ariel D. Procaccia, Benjamin Schiffer, Shirley Zhang
